competencies.raw: Recognize personal worldviews and perspectives, considering their influence on values, actions, and preferred futures; analyze internal and external factors to inform personal career-life choices for post-graduation planning; assess personal transferable skills, and identify strengths and those requiring refinement; explore and evaluate personal strategies to maintain well-being.
i_can_statements: I can recognize my own worldview and how my values, cultural background, and experiences shape my vision of a preferred future.
I can analyze both internal factors (strengths, values, interests) and external factors (labour market, family, community) in making post-graduation plans.
I can honestly assess my transferable skills — identifying genuine strengths and areas I need to continue developing.
I can evaluate the well-being strategies I have developed and articulate how I will maintain balance in my post-graduation life.

competencies.raw: Collaborate with a mentor to inform career-life development and exploration; engage with personal, education, and employment networks to cultivate post-graduation resources and social capital; create and critique personal and public profiles for self-advocacy and marketing; demonstrate and reflect on inclusive, respectful, and safe interactions in multiple career-life contexts.
i_can_statements: I can collaborate with a mentor to clarify my post-graduation direction and develop my Capstone Project.
I can actively engage with personal, educational, and professional networks to cultivate resources and social capital for life after graduation.
I can create and critically evaluate professional profiles — resume, LinkedIn, portfolio, and digital presence — for self-advocacy and career marketing.
I can demonstrate and reflect on professional, inclusive, and safe conduct across diverse career-life contexts.

competencies.raw: Explore possibilities for preferred personal and education/employment futures; identify and apply preferred approaches to learning for ongoing development; engage in, reflect on, and evaluate career-life exploration.
i_can_statements: I can explore and articulate clear possibilities for my preferred post-graduation future — with specific goals and pathways.
I can identify my preferred approaches to learning and apply them in pursuing post-graduation growth.
I can engage in authentic career-life exploration and critically evaluate what I have learned about myself and the world of work.

competencies.raw: Reflect on experiences and assess Core Competencies development; design, assemble, and present a Capstone Project.
i_can_statements: I can reflect honestly and deeply on my experiences across all subject areas and activities and assess my Core Competencies development.
I can design, assemble, and present a Capstone Project that tells the story of my learning journey and demonstrates my readiness for life after graduation.
I can present my Capstone to an authentic audience — including community members and post-secondary representatives — with confidence and clarity.

competencies.raw: Analyze how writers make choices about form, structure, and style in response to rhetorical situation
i_can_statements: I can identify the purpose, audience, and context for various texts
I can analyze how writers adapt their approach—tone, structure, evidence—to different audiences and situations
I can explain how genre conventions shape what writers do in particular writing forms
I can identify and discuss stylistic choices and their effects on readers

competencies.raw: Understand effective argumentation, including how writers develop claims, support arguments with evidence, and address counterarguments
i_can_statements: I can identify thesis statements and supporting arguments in persuasive texts
I can evaluate the quality and relevance of evidence used in arguments
I can recognize logical fallacies and assess their impact on argument strength
I can analyze how writers acknowledge and respond to opposing viewpoints

competencies.raw: Analyze how writers integrate research, sources, and evidence to develop well-supported writing
i_can_statements: I can identify how writers use different types of evidence and source material
I can evaluate the credibility and relevance of sources
I can analyze how writers synthesize information from multiple sources
I can examine how writers acknowledge sources and maintain academic integrity

competencies.raw: Develop clear, well-supported thesis statements and construct coherent, logical arguments
i_can_statements: I can write focused thesis statements that respond to writing prompts or questions
I can organize ideas logically and ensure each paragraph supports my overall argument
I can provide specific, relevant evidence that supports my claims
I can explain the significance and implications of my argument

competencies.raw: Conduct research, locate and evaluate sources, and integrate research into writing appropriately
i_can_statements: I can identify reliable sources appropriate for my research questions
I can evaluate sources for credibility, bias, and relevance
I can take useful notes and organize research findings
I can integrate quotations, paraphrases, and summaries smoothly into my writing with proper citations

competencies.raw: Develop distinctive voice and style through deliberate word choice, syntax, and tone
i_can_statements: I can make strategic word choices that enhance clarity and impact
I can vary sentence structure to control pace and emphasis
I can adopt appropriate tone for different writing situations
I can develop more distinctive style through reading, practice, and revision

competencies.raw: Revise and edit extensively to strengthen clarity, coherence, argumentation, and correctness
i_can_statements: I can revise for coherence and logical flow of ideas
I can strengthen arguments and evidence through substantial revision
I can edit for sentence-level clarity and effectiveness
I can proofread carefully for grammar, mechanics, and formatting conventions

competencies.raw: Read for enjoyment and personal goals; understand and appreciate First Peoples perspectives, diversity, and land/place influence.
i_can_statements: I can read challenging and diverse creative texts for both pleasure and craft analysis.
I can engage with First Peoples creative writing with cultural awareness and genuine literary appreciation.
I can analyze how land and place function as creative themes and formal elements in literature.

competencies.raw: Use information for diverse purposes; evaluate relevance, accuracy, and reliability; understand how forms and features reflect purposes; think critically and creatively to analyze ideas.
i_can_statements: I can conduct research to inform creative work — for setting, character, historical period, or subject matter.
I can analyze mentor texts at a sophisticated craft level, extracting applicable techniques.
I can evaluate my own creative work with the same critical rigor I apply to published writing.

competencies.raw: Identify and understand contexts, values, and perspectives; understand how language constructs identities; construct connections; evaluate literary elements.
i_can_statements: I can analyze how my own identity and positionality shape what I write and how.
I can recognize when my creative work reproduces stereotypes or limited perspectives — and revise accordingly.
I can engage respectfully and authentically with identities and experiences different from my own in my writing.

competencies.raw: Respectfully exchange ideas from diverse perspectives to build shared understanding and transform thinking; respond to text in personal, creative, and critical ways.
i_can_statements: I can contribute substantively to a senior writer's workshop — giving nuanced, specific, useful feedback.
I can receive feedback with openness and use it to make genuine revisions.
I can write responses to published creative work that demonstrate sophisticated craft awareness.

competencies.raw: Select and apply speaking and listening skills in varied contexts; use writing and design processes; express and support opinions with evidence; assess and refine texts; manipulate language purposefully; use figurative and literal language; use writers' practices.
i_can_statements: I can write a craft statement that articulates and defends the artistic choices in a finished piece.
I can manipulate language with precision and intention — using figurative, imagistic, and rhythmic resources purposefully.
I can demonstrate the habits of an active writing practice: regular drafting, revision, and reading.

competencies.raw: Use Canadian conventions proficiently; use acknowledgements and citations; transform ideas to create original texts using varied genres, forms, structures, and styles.
i_can_statements: I can produce a portfolio of polished original creative work in multiple forms.
I can prepare work for submission to journals, contests, or performance with appropriate professional conventions.
I can acknowledge all literary influences, sources, and oral traditions that have shaped my work.

competencies.raw: Analyze how First Peoples languages and texts reflect their cultures, knowledge, histories, and worldviews.
i_can_statements: I can analyze how a First Peoples text reflects the specific culture, knowledge system, and worldview of its community of origin.
I can explain how First Peoples languages carry knowledge that cannot be fully translated.
I can identify how colonization has affected First Peoples languages and what language revitalization means.

competencies.raw: Access information for diverse purposes from various sources; evaluate relevance, accuracy, and reliability; apply comprehension strategies; analyze how different forms and features reflect purposes, audiences, and messages.
i_can_statements: I can locate and evaluate First Peoples texts from a wide range of sources — community archives, academic presses, oral recordings.
I can apply sustained close reading to complex First Peoples texts.
I can analyze how a First Peoples writer uses form, structure, and genre to serve cultural and political purposes.

competencies.raw: Think critically, creatively, and reflectively; recognize personal, social, and cultural contexts; understand how language constructs and reflects identities; construct meaningful personal connections; evaluate literary elements; analyze diversity within First Peoples societies; assess authenticity; analyze influence of land/place; examine significance of First Peoples language terms; discern nuanced word meanings; identify bias, contradictions, distortions, and omissions.
i_can_statements: I can assess whether a text is an authentic First Peoples voice or a colonial representation.
I can analyze how land and territory function in a First Peoples text — spiritually, politically, and aesthetically.
I can examine the significance of untranslated First Peoples language terms in English-language texts.
I can identify stereotypes, distortions, or omissions in representations of First Peoples in any text.
I can discuss diversity within and across First Peoples societies as represented in their texts.

competencies.raw: Respectfully exchange ideas and viewpoints from diverse perspectives; demonstrate speaking and listening skills; select and apply appropriate oral communication formats; express and support opinions with evidence.
i_can_statements: I can engage in respectful dialogue about First Peoples texts, listening deeply and sharing thoughtfully.
I can participate in oral formats that reflect First Peoples communication traditions (e.g., talking circles, storytelling).
I can argue for an interpretation of a First Peoples text with specific, well-chosen evidence.

competencies.raw: Respond to texts personally, creatively, and critically; use writing and design processes; assess and refine texts; experiment with genres, forms, and styles.
i_can_statements: I can write a personal response that connects my own experience and identity to a First Peoples text.
I can create original writing — poetry, story, essay — inspired by First Peoples literary traditions.
I can write a critical analysis that situates a First Peoples text within its cultural and historical context.

competencies.raw: Use conventions of First Peoples and Canadian spelling, syntax, and diction appropriately; transform ideas and information to create original texts; recognize intellectual property rights and community protocols.
i_can_statements: I can apply both standard Canadian and First Peoples writing conventions appropriately.
I can produce original texts inspired by First Peoples literary traditions with cultural awareness and respect.
I can acknowledge sources, communities, and oral traditions that inform my work.
I can explain community protocols for using First Peoples oral stories and cultural material.

competencies.raw: Read for enjoyment and achievement; recognize First Peoples perspectives, diversity, and land/place influence.

I can analyze First Peoples texts with full attention to their cultural context, oral tradition, and relationship to land.
I can explain the legal status of First Peoples oral tradition in Canada and its significance.

competencies.raw: Evaluate relevance, accuracy, and reliability of texts; apply comprehension strategies in varied contexts to guide inquiry and transform thinking.
i_can_statements: I can evaluate secondary sources about literature with critical awareness of perspective and bias.
I can apply sophisticated reading strategies to transform my understanding of complex texts.
I can guide a rigorous literary inquiry using self-generated questions.

competencies.raw: Understand and appreciate how different forms and features reflect purposes and messages; think critically and creatively to analyze ideas within, between, and beyond texts.
i_can_statements: I can analyze how a text's form and structure are inseparable from its meaning.
I can synthesize ideas across multiple texts to construct a complex literary argument.
I can examine a text's assumptions, silences, and ideological positions.

competencies.raw: Recognize and analyze personal, social, cultural contexts; appreciate how language constructs identities; construct meaningful personal connections; analyze literary elements; discern nuanced word meanings; identify bias, contradictions, omissions.
i_can_statements: I can analyze how race, gender, class, and colonialism shape both the texts I read and my reading of them.
I can trace the connotative and denotative weight of specific language choices in a sophisticated text.
I can identify what a text omits or silences and explain the ideological significance of those omissions.

competencies.raw: Respectfully exchange ideas from diverse perspectives to build shared understanding and transform thinking.
i_can_statements: I can participate in sophisticated literary dialogue, building on others' ideas and offering my own.
I can represent and engage fairly with interpretations I disagree with.
I can contribute to collective intellectual growth through rigorous discussion.

competencies.raw: Respond to text personally, creatively, and critically; select and apply appropriate speaking and listening skills; demonstrate formal and informal communication.
i_can_statements: I can write literary responses that are analytically sophisticated and personally authentic.
I can communicate ideas clearly and persuasively in formal academic and informal conversational contexts.
I can deliver a polished oral presentation on a literary or cultural topic.

competencies.raw: Use writing and design processes; express and support opinions with evidence to achieve purpose; reflect, assess, and refine texts.
i_can_statements: I can write a sophisticated literary analysis essay with a clear argument, specific evidence, and nuanced commentary.
I can revise my writing for clarity, sophistication, and impact through multiple drafts.
I can produce writing that meets the demands of post-secondary academic contexts.

competencies.raw: Use Canadian spelling, grammar, and punctuation proficiently; use citations; transform ideas to create original texts using varied genres, forms, structures, and styles.
i_can_statements: I can apply conventions of Canadian spelling, grammar, and punctuation consistently and contextually.
I can cite all literary and research sources accurately using MLA or another accepted format.
I can produce original texts — analytical, creative, multimodal — that demonstrate senior-level sophistication.

competencies.raw: Read for enjoyment and personal goals; understand and appreciate First Peoples perspectives, diversity, and land/place influence.
i_can_statements: I can read challenging literary texts for both pleasure and rigorous intellectual engagement.
I can analyze First Peoples texts with full understanding of their cultural, legal, and historical context.
I can analyze how land, place, and territory function in First Peoples literature.

competencies.raw: Use information for diverse purposes; evaluate relevance, accuracy, and reliability; select and apply appropriate strategies to comprehend, guide inquiry, and transform thinking.
i_can_statements: I can conduct literary research using scholarly databases, critical editions, and academic journals.
I can apply multiple reading strategies to transform my understanding of complex or resistant texts.
I can follow a rigorous literary inquiry from question to argument to evidence.

competencies.raw: Understand and appreciate how different forms and features reflect purposes; think critically and creatively to analyze ideas within, between, and beyond texts.
i_can_statements: I can conduct a comparative analysis of two or more literary texts, identifying thematic and formal connections.
I can analyze how a text's genre and form are inseparable from its ideological content.
I can synthesize ideas from multiple texts and sources into a complex, original argument.

competencies.raw: Recognize and analyze personal, social, cultural contexts; understand how language constructs identities; construct connections; analyze literary elements; recognize text structures; discern nuanced word meanings.
i_can_statements: I can apply concepts from literary theory (post-colonialism, feminism, New Historicism) to my analysis.
I can trace the nuanced semantic weight of specific words across a literary text.
I can analyze how a text's structure — its form of beginning, middle, and end — is itself meaningful.

competencies.raw: Respectfully exchange ideas from diverse perspectives to build shared understanding and transform thinking.
i_can_statements: I can engage in sophisticated literary dialogue, building genuinely on others' ideas.
I can represent and fairly test interpretations different from my own.
I can articulate complex literary ideas clearly in both conversation and writing.

competencies.raw: Respond to text personally, creatively, and critically; select and apply speaking and listening skills for varied formal and informal contexts; use writing and design processes.
i_can_statements: I can write literary analyses that are both intellectually rigorous and personally engaged.
I can present a literary argument persuasively in formal oral or written contexts.
I can produce diverse literary response forms: analysis, comparison, personal essay, creative response.

competencies.raw: Express and support opinions with evidence; reflect, assess, and refine texts; use conventions proficiently; use citations; transform ideas to create original texts.
i_can_statements: I can write a sophisticated literary essay with a nuanced thesis, specific evidence, and analytical commentary.
I can revise my analytical writing to eliminate vagueness, improve argument structure, and increase precision.
I can produce original literary texts that demonstrate senior-level understanding of craft.

competencies.raw: Critically analyze complex digital media ecosystems for power, equity, and democratic implications; evaluate advanced questions of AI-generated content, platform governance, and digital rights; synthesize diverse perspectives on the role of media in society.
i_can_statements: I can conduct sophisticated analysis of digital media ecosystems, examining platform governance, algorithmic power, and media monopolies.
I can evaluate emerging issues — AI content generation, deepfakes, digital rights, and data colonialism — using ethical and critical frameworks.
I can synthesize diverse scholarly, journalistic, and community perspectives on the social role of digital media.
I can connect Indigenous digital sovereignty to global questions of media equity and self-determination.

competencies.raw: Produce a substantial portfolio of original digital work demonstrating mastery across multiple media forms; demonstrate professional-level technical skill, creative vision, and ethical responsibility; present and defend creative work with articulate critical reflection.
i_can_statements: I can produce a substantial, polished portfolio of original digital work across multiple media forms.
I can demonstrate professional-level technical skill in at least two digital production domains.
I can articulate and defend my creative vision and the ethical choices embedded in my media work.
I can contribute meaningfully to digital communities and public conversations as a responsible media maker.
I can reflect critically on my development as a digital communicator and set goals for post-graduation growth.

competencies.raw: Analyze and evaluate the craft, structure, and impact of complex oral texts; synthesize perspectives from diverse oral traditions; critically evaluate the relationship between spoken language, power, and identity.
i_can_statements: I can analyze and evaluate the craft, structure, and rhetorical effectiveness of complex speeches, performances, and oral texts.
I can synthesize insights from diverse oral traditions — including Indigenous, political, artistic, and community voices.
I can critically examine how spoken language reflects, constructs, and challenges power dynamics and social norms.
I can connect oral communication to questions of identity, justice, and civic responsibility.

competencies.raw: Create and deliver sophisticated oral texts demonstrating mastery of rhetorical craft and personal voice; lead and facilitate complex oral communication contexts; reflect critically on personal growth as a communicator.
i_can_statements: I can create and deliver polished, purposeful oral presentations and performances that demonstrate rhetorical mastery.
I can lead and facilitate complex oral communication contexts — debates, seminars, community forums.
I can use spoken language as a tool for advocacy and social change.
I can reflect critically and specifically on my growth as a communicator and articulate clear ongoing goals.
I can mentor peers in developing their oral communication skills.

competencies.raw: Use logic and patterns to solve puzzles and play games; use reasoning and logic to explore, analyze, and apply mathematical ideas; estimate reasonably; demonstrate and apply mental math strategies; use tools or technology to explore and create patterns and relationships; model mathematics in contextualized experiences.
i_can_statements: I can apply logical reasoning to analyze and solve trades and technical mathematics problems.
I can estimate quantities, measurements, and costs in workplace contexts and evaluate the reasonableness of results.
I can model mathematical relationships using diagrams, tables, formulas, and technology.
I can recognize and apply mathematical patterns in trades and technical applications.

competencies.raw: Apply multiple strategies to solve problems in both abstract and contextualized situations; develop, demonstrate, and apply mathematical understanding through play, inquiry, and problem solving; visualize to explore mathematical concepts; engage in problem-solving experiences that are connected to place, story, cultural practices, and perspectives.
i_can_statements: I can apply multiple strategies to solve measurement, geometry, and financial problems in trades contexts.
I can visualize and sketch geometric problems before solving them.
I can connect mathematical problems to real workplace scenarios, including Indigenous land-based contexts.
I can develop and test solution strategies for unfamiliar applied problems.

competencies.raw: Use mathematical vocabulary and language to contribute to mathematical discussions; explain and justify mathematical ideas and decisions; communicate mathematical thinking in many ways; represent mathematical ideas in concrete, pictorial, and symbolic forms.
i_can_statements: I can explain my mathematical thinking and justify my solutions using precise trades vocabulary and mathematical language.
I can represent mathematical problems and solutions using sketches, formulas, tables, and technology.
I can communicate mathematical ideas clearly in professional and workplace-relevant contexts.

competencies.raw: Reflect on mathematical thinking; connect mathematical concepts to each other and to other areas and personal interests; use mathematical arguments to support personal choices; incorporate First Peoples worldviews and perspectives to make connections to mathematical concepts.
i_can_statements: I can connect mathematical concepts to trades, technical fields, and personal career interests.
I can reflect on the effectiveness of my mathematical strategies and identify improvements.
I can recognize mathematical knowledge embedded in First Peoples practices and connect it to course concepts.
I can use mathematical reasoning to support informed personal and professional decisions.

competencies.raw: Develop thinking strategies to solve puzzles and play games; explore, analyze, and apply mathematical ideas using reason, technology, and tools; estimate reasonably and demonstrate fluent, flexible thinking about number; model with mathematics in situational contexts; think creatively with curiosity.
i_can_statements: I can develop and apply thinking strategies to evaluate limits, derivatives, and integrals.
I can use graphing technology to explore limits, continuity, derivatives, and antiderivatives.
I can estimate instantaneous rates of change and accumulated quantities from graphs and tables.
I can model real-world rates of change and accumulation problems using calculus.

competencies.raw: Develop conceptual understanding through play, story, inquiry, and problem solving; visualize to explore and illustrate mathematical concepts; apply flexible and strategic approaches to solve problems; solve with persistence and positive disposition; engage in place-based problem-solving.
i_can_statements: I can develop deep conceptual understanding of limits, derivatives, and integrals through inquiry.
I can visualize the relationship between a function and its derivative graphically.
I can apply differentiation and integration rules flexibly, including chain rule and substitution.
I can solve optimization and related rates problems systematically and with persistence.

competencies.raw: Explain and justify mathematical ideas and decisions in multiple ways; represent mathematical ideas in concrete, pictorial, and symbolic forms; use mathematical vocabulary in discussions; take risks when offering ideas.
i_can_statements: I can explain and justify limit evaluations, differentiation steps, and integration processes clearly.
I can represent calculus concepts graphically, numerically, analytically, and verbally.
I can use precise calculus notation: lim, d/dx, f'(x), ∫, dx, and the fundamental theorem.

competencies.raw: Reflect on mathematical thinking; connect mathematical concepts with each other and personal interests; use mistakes as opportunities to advance learning; incorporate First Peoples worldviews and practices.
i_can_statements: I can reflect on my calculus reasoning and identify conceptual connections between limits, derivatives, and integrals.
I can connect calculus to physics (velocity, acceleration), biology (population growth), and economics (marginal analysis).
I can incorporate First Peoples perspectives on natural rates of change and cumulative processes into mathematical discussions.

competencies.raw: Analyze algorithms and data structures for efficiency and apply design patterns to model complex systems.
i_can_statements: I can analyze the time and space complexity of algorithms using Big O notation.
I can compare data structures and select appropriate ones based on performance requirements.
I can recognize common design patterns and apply them to solve recurring design problems.
I can model complex systems through object hierarchies and relationships.

competencies.raw: Implement object-oriented programs with proper encapsulation, inheritance, and polymorphism to solve complex problems.
i_can_statements: I can design and implement classes with appropriate attributes, methods, and access control.
I can create inheritance hierarchies and use polymorphism to write flexible, reusable code.
I can implement and use advanced data structures like trees, graphs, and heaps.
I can debug complex programs and optimize code for efficiency and readability.

competencies.raw: Communicate software design and architecture through documentation, diagrams, and clear code.
i_can_statements: I can create and interpret UML class diagrams representing object structures and relationships.
I can write comprehensive documentation explaining design decisions and usage.
I can refactor code to improve readability while maintaining functionality.
I can present software designs and explain architectural choices to other programmers.

competencies.raw: Reflect on the broader impacts of technology and the ethical responsibilities of software developers.
i_can_statements: I can identify and discuss ethical issues in software design including privacy, bias, and accessibility.
I can explain how computing relates to environmental sustainability and resource consumption.
I can recognize the social impact of technology choices and consider diverse perspectives.
I can apply ethical principles to make responsible decisions in software development.

competencies.raw: Develop thinking strategies to solve puzzles and play games; explore, analyze, and apply mathematical ideas using reason, technology, and other tools; estimate reasonably; model with mathematics in situational contexts; think creatively with curiosity.
i_can_statements: I can develop thinking strategies to solve complex counting, probability, and function problems.
I can use graphing technology to explore and model function families and regression analysis.
I can model real-world situations using appropriate function families and probability tools.

competencies.raw: Develop, demonstrate, and apply mathematical understanding through play, story, inquiry, and problem solving; visualize to explore and illustrate mathematical concepts; apply flexible and strategic approaches; solve with persistence and positive disposition; engage in place-based problem-solving.
i_can_statements: I can develop and demonstrate understanding of combinatorics, functions, and probability through inquiry.
I can visualize graphs of polynomial, exponential, logarithmic, and sinusoidal functions.
I can apply flexible strategies to solve probability, regression, and financial problems.

competencies.raw: Explain and justify mathematical ideas and decisions in many ways; represent mathematical ideas in concrete, pictorial, and symbolic forms; use mathematical vocabulary; take risks when offering ideas.
i_can_statements: I can explain and justify probability calculations, function models, and financial decisions.
I can represent functions using graphs, equations, and tables, and choose appropriate forms for context.
I can use accurate mathematical vocabulary in discussions of probability, statistics, and functions.

competencies.raw: Reflect on mathematical thinking; connect concepts with each other, other areas, and personal interests; use mistakes as opportunities to advance learning; incorporate First Peoples worldviews, perspectives, knowledge, and practices.
i_can_statements: I can reflect on my probability and function modelling thinking and refine my strategies.
I can connect mathematics to science, economics, and personal financial planning.
I can incorporate First Peoples geometric and mathematical knowledge — including fractal-like patterns in Indigenous art and seasonal cycles — into my thinking.

competencies.raw: Sustain intellectual curiosity; make observations to identify questions; formulate multiple hypotheses.
i_can_statements: I can formulate complex geological questions about earth materials, geological history, and dynamic Earth processes.
I can make careful field and laboratory observations and generate testable geological hypotheses.
I can predict geological outcomes based on knowledge of mineral chemistry, tectonic forces, and weathering processes.

competencies.raw: Plan and select investigation methods; assess risks; use SI units and digital technologies; apply accuracy and precision concepts.
i_can_statements: I can plan geological investigations — field mapping, sample collection, and laboratory analysis.
I can assess safety and ethical considerations in field and laboratory geology work.
I can use appropriate tools: hand lens, streak plate, acid test, compass, and digital mapping technologies.
I can apply accuracy and precision in geological measurements and geologic time calculations.

categories.name: Processing and Analyzing Data and Information

competencies.raw: Classify and interpret data; apply First Peoples perspectives; analyze patterns and relationships; construct and interpret models.
i_can_statements: I can apply First Peoples knowledge of landscapes, landforms, and geological events to enrich geological analysis.
I can classify and interpret mineral and rock samples using physical and chemical properties.
I can construct and interpret geological cross-sections, maps, and stratigraphic columns.
I can analyze patterns in geological data including seismic records, radiometric dating results, and tectonic maps.

competencies.raw: Evaluate methods and identify errors; improve investigation quality; assess validity of models; consider ethical implications.
i_can_statements: I can evaluate the methods and limitations of geological dating techniques and stratigraphic interpretation.
I can assess the validity of geological models including plate tectonic reconstructions.
I can consider the ethical, environmental, and Indigenous rights implications of resource extraction in BC.

competencies.raw: Contribute to community solutions; design collaborative projects; implement multiple problem-solving strategies.
i_can_statements: I can apply geological knowledge to real-world problems including mineral exploration, hazard assessment, and environmental management.
I can connect geology learning to resource industry careers and geoscience pathways.
I can consider how geological knowledge supports sustainable resource management and Indigenous land stewardship.

competencies.raw: Formulate theoretical models; communicate scientific ideas with evidence; express perspectives through place-based connections.
i_can_statements: I can communicate geological interpretations using accurate terminology, maps, and cross-sections.
I can present evidence-based geological arguments about the history and structure of a region.
I can express how BC's geological landscape — and First Peoples relationships to it — shape my understanding of geology.

competencies.raw: Develop thinking strategies to solve puzzles and games; explore, analyze, and apply mathematical ideas using reason, technology, and tools; estimate reasonably and demonstrate fluent, flexible, strategic thinking about numbers; model mathematics in situational contexts; think creatively with curiosity and wonder.
i_can_statements: I can develop sophisticated algebraic thinking strategies to solve complex function and equation problems.
I can use graphing technology (Desmos, graphing calculator) to explore transformations and function families.
I can estimate and verify the reasonableness of algebraic results, including function values and roots.
I can model real-world phenomena using appropriate function families.

competencies.raw: Develop conceptual understanding through play, story, inquiry, and problem solving; visualize to explore mathematical concepts and relationships; apply flexible and strategic approaches to solve problems; solve with persistence and positive disposition; engage in place-based problem-solving.
i_can_statements: I can develop deep conceptual understanding of function behaviour through inquiry and exploration.
I can visualize function graphs — including asymptotes, discontinuities, and transformations — accurately.
I can apply multiple algebraic strategies — logarithmic solving, polynomial division, trigonometric identities — flexibly.

competencies.raw: Explain and justify mathematical ideas and decisions in many ways; represent mathematical ideas in concrete, pictorial, and symbolic forms; use mathematical vocabulary in discussions; take risks when offering ideas in discourse.
i_can_statements: I can explain and justify algebraic transformations, function analyses, and solution strategies.
I can represent functions accurately in multiple forms: equation, graph, table, and verbal description.
I can use precise Pre-calculus vocabulary: asymptote, discontinuity, period, amplitude, and inverse function.

competencies.raw: Reflect on mathematical thinking; connect mathematical concepts with each other, other areas, and personal interests; use mistakes as opportunities to advance learning; incorporate First Peoples worldviews, perspectives, knowledge, and practices.
i_can_statements: I can reflect on my function analysis and algebraic thinking and identify patterns across function families.
I can connect Pre-calculus concepts to physics, chemistry, and computer science applications.
I can incorporate First Peoples mathematical knowledge and perspectives — including cyclical thinking and traditional geometric patterns — into my work.

competencies.raw: Design data collection methods and select appropriate statistical approaches based on research questions.
i_can_statements: I can identify population, sample, and variables in statistical contexts.
I can distinguish between observational studies and experiments and recognize their different limitations.
I can design sampling strategies and explain how randomness reduces bias.
I can identify sources of bias and confounding variables in studies and explain their effects.

competencies.raw: Calculate descriptive statistics, work with probability distributions, and conduct hypothesis tests to draw conclusions.
i_can_statements: I can calculate and interpret measures of center, spread, and position for distributions.
I can use probability distributions to calculate probabilities and make predictions.
I can conduct hypothesis tests and interpret p-values and confidence intervals correctly.
I can create and interpret regression models and use them to make predictions with appropriate caution.

competencies.raw: Represent data visually and communicate statistical findings with clarity and appropriate context.
i_can_statements: I can create and interpret histograms, box plots, scatter plots, and other statistical graphs.
I can describe distributions using shape, center, and spread.
I can present statistical results with proper limitations and appropriate language about uncertainty.
I can critique graphs and statistical claims for accuracy, bias, and appropriate representation.

competencies.raw: Reflect on how statistical thinking applies across disciplines and consider ethical dimensions of data collection and analysis.
i_can_statements: I can explain why understanding statistics is essential in a data-driven world.
I can identify misuses of statistics in media and marketing and explain what makes them misleading.
I can discuss ethical considerations in data collection and the importance of informed consent and privacy.
I can apply statistical thinking to evaluate claims and make informed decisions in personal and professional contexts.

competencies.raw: Develop thinking strategies; explore and apply mathematical ideas using reason, technology, and tools; estimate reasonably; model with mathematics in situational contexts; think creatively with curiosity.
i_can_statements: I can develop and apply thinking strategies to solve practical and workplace mathematical problems.
I can model real-world financial, measurement, and statistical situations with mathematics.
I can estimate reasonably and judge whether results make sense in context.
I can approach unfamiliar problems creatively using reasoning and available tools.

competencies.raw: Develop and apply conceptual understanding through inquiry and problem solving; visualize mathematical concepts; apply flexible and strategic approaches; solve with persistence; engage in place-based problem-solving.
i_can_statements: I can apply mathematical understanding to solve complex financial and measurement problems.
I can visualize 3D objects and interpret technical drawings.
I can select and apply multiple strategies — proportional reasoning, formulas, technology — to solve practical problems.

competencies.raw: Explain and justify mathematical ideas in many ways; represent ideas in concrete, pictorial, and symbolic forms; use mathematical vocabulary; take risks in discourse.
i_can_statements: I can clearly explain and justify mathematical reasoning in practical and workplace contexts.
I can represent financial and measurement solutions using tables, diagrams, graphs, and written explanations.
I can communicate confidently using appropriate mathematical vocabulary.

competencies.raw: Reflect on mathematical thinking; connect concepts with each other and personal interests; use mistakes as learning opportunities; incorporate First Peoples worldviews, perspectives, knowledge, and practices.
i_can_statements: I can reflect on my mathematical approaches and identify improvements.
I can connect mathematics to career planning, small business, and independent living.
I can incorporate First Peoples knowledge and practices, including traditional measurement and resource management, into my thinking.

competencies.raw: Plan and lead activity sessions; demonstrate and teach movement skills; provide constructive feedback; adapt teaching for different learners; create inclusive and safe learning environments.
i_can_statements: I can plan and lead physical activity sessions that are engaging, appropriately challenging, and aligned with learning objectives.
I can clearly demonstrate movement techniques and explain key coaching cues that help learners understand and execute skills.
I can assess learner performance, identify technical issues or misconceptions, and provide specific, actionable feedback.
I can adapt teaching methods, activity complexity, and communication style to support diverse learners — including those with different abilities, learning styles, and confidence levels.
I can create physically and psychologically safe learning environments where all participants feel respected, valued, and encouraged to challenge themselves.

competencies.raw: Design programs for diverse groups; understand community physical activity needs and assets; collaborate with community partners; advocate for physical activity access and equity.
i_can_statements: I can design inclusive physical activity programs that welcome and engage people of different ages, abilities, backgrounds, and identities.
I can assess community physical activity needs, identify barriers to participation, and design programs that address these barriers.
I can identify and engage community resources — facilities, organizations, volunteers, funding — that support physical activity programs.
I can advocate for policies and conditions that increase physical activity access and opportunity for all community members.
I can evaluate programs for effectiveness and make adjustments based on participant feedback and observed outcomes.

competencies.raw: Understand barriers to physical activity participation; adapt activities and environments for accessibility; support people with different abilities and health conditions; promote equity in physical activity.
i_can_statements: I can identify and articulate barriers that prevent people from participating in physical activity — including social, economic, accessibility, and systemic barriers.
I can modify activities, environments, and instruction to ensure people of all abilities can participate meaningfully.
I can work supportively with people who have different physical abilities, chronic health conditions, mental health challenges, or other factors affecting activity.
I can examine how gender, race, socioeconomic status, and other identity factors affect physical activity access and experience.
I can advocate for and implement practical changes that increase physical activity equity and inclusion in schools and communities.

categories.name: Lifetime Physical Activity Planning and Community Contribution

competencies.raw: Support others in lifetime activity planning; contribute to school and community physical activity initiatives; understand career pathways in physical activity and wellness; model sustained personal commitment.
i_can_statements: I can guide peers in assessing their activity interests, setting realistic goals, and developing sustainable long-term plans for physical activity.
I can actively contribute to school physical activity initiatives — whether through program design, leadership, coaching, or advocacy.
I can engage with community physical activity programs and organizations, and contribute meaningfully to their missions.
I can articulate my own sustained commitment to physical activity and explain how it shapes my identity and choices.
I can explore careers in physical activity leadership — coaching, recreation, fitness instruction, athletic training, and health promotion.

competencies.raw: Design macrocycles, mesocycles, and microcycles; integrate multiple training qualities; manage training load and fatigue; apply periodization to competitive sport and fitness development; adapt plans based on performance data.
i_can_statements: I can design a multi-year macrocyclic training plan that strategically phases development, peaking, and recovery across seasons or competition schedules.
I can structure mesocycles (4-6 week blocks) with progressive overload, adaptation phases, and recovery emphasis aligned with overall goals.
I can integrate multiple fitness qualities — strength, power, endurance, agility, technique — within a coherent periodic structure.
I can manage cumulative training load, monitor fatigue and readiness, and adjust plans to prevent overtraining and optimize adaptation.
I can analyze performance data and athlete feedback to modify plans in response to actual adaptation and progress.

competencies.raw: Understand physiological training adaptations; apply sports science to program design; analyze training demands; predict performance outcomes; use data to guide training modifications.
i_can_statements: I can explain how different training stimuli trigger physiological adaptations — including muscle hypertrophy, aerobic capacity, neural efficiency, and metabolic changes.
I can analyze the specific physiological demands of different sports — energy systems, muscle groups, movement patterns, and conditioning requirements.
I can design training that specifically targets the physiological demands of a sport, progressively building required capacities.
I can interpret performance data — lactate thresholds, VO2 measurements, power outputs, biomechanics — to assess training effects and guide program decisions.
I can predict likely performance outcomes based on training progression, fitness levels, and historical data patterns.

competencies.raw: Analyze sport-specific demands; design sport-specific training programs; integrate technique and conditioning; train energy systems appropriately; apply sport-specific testing and assessment.
i_can_statements: I can comprehensively analyze the physiological, biomechanical, and cognitive demands of a specific sport or athletic context.
I can design training programs that directly develop the capacities most important for performance in a specific sport.
I can integrate technical skill development with physical conditioning, ensuring both develop appropriately together.
I can select and apply appropriate training methods for the energy systems critical to performance — aerobic, anaerobic alactic, anaerobic lactic.
I can use sport-specific performance tests and metrics to assess fitness development and guide training modifications.

competencies.raw: Understand common injury mechanisms; apply injury prevention strategies; recognize contraindications; guide return-to-play decisions; understand rehabilitation principles; support athletes through injury.
i_can_statements: I can identify movement patterns and training practices that increase injury risk, and design modifications to reduce injury probability.
I can recognize muscle imbalances, flexibility limitations, or movement dysfunctions that predispose to injury.
I can implement injury prevention training — including proprioceptive training, dynamic stability work, and movement quality focus.
I can understand basic rehabilitation principles and work collaboratively with healthcare providers in guiding return-to-sport progressions.
I can support athletes emotionally and psychologically through injury, and help maintain fitness during rehabilitation constraints.

competencies.raw: Plan comprehensive expeditions including logistics, safety systems, and learning outcomes; lead expeditions with effective group management; mentor peers in wilderness skills; make sound decisions in changing conditions.
i_can_statements: I can plan a multi-day wilderness expedition comprehensively — including route analysis, supply logistics, shelter and cooking systems, weather preparation, and contingency planning.
I can establish and maintain safety systems for group expeditions — including hazard communication, emergency protocols, and continuous risk monitoring.
I can manage group dynamics, resolve conflicts, and maintain team cohesion and morale across multi-day expeditions.
I can mentor peers in wilderness skills, providing skilled instruction and encouragement while supporting their learning and confidence development.
I can make sound decisions in dynamic outdoor situations, balancing safety with meaningful challenge, consulting team input, and adjusting plans based on changing conditions.

competencies.raw: Demonstrate expert-level wilderness skills; navigate complex terrain confidently; respond effectively to wilderness emergencies; adapt skills across varied environments and seasons.
i_can_statements: I can demonstrate expert-level proficiency across core wilderness skills — navigation, campcraft, water crossing, risk assessment, and emergency response.
I can navigate confidently through complex terrain using maps, compass, and landscape interpretation — including night navigation and poor-visibility situations.
I can establish and maintain safe camps in challenging conditions, including winter camping, alpine environments, and remote locations.
I can respond effectively to wilderness emergencies using wilderness first aid, appropriate equipment, and sound judgment.
I can adapt my skills across seasons, environments, and expedition types — from summer backpacking to winter mountaineering to water-based expeditions.

competencies.raw: Understand BC ecosystems and ecological processes; apply First Peoples ecological knowledge; recognize environmental impacts; contribute to conservation and environmental stewardship.
i_can_statements: I can identify and describe BC's major ecosystems — coastal temperate rainforests, interior forests, alpine zones, and wetlands — and understand their characteristics.
I can explain ecological processes including nutrient cycling, succession, predator-prey relationships, and human impacts on ecosystems.
I can recognize and interpret First Peoples ecological knowledge about land stewardship, sustainable resource use, and reciprocal relationships with nature.
I can assess human impacts on wilderness areas — from recreation impacts to climate change — and discuss conservation strategies.
I can contribute to conservation work — habitat restoration, invasive species management, or environmental monitoring — during outdoor experiences.

competencies.raw: Design and lead outdoor education programs; understand outdoor therapy and transformative learning; advocate for outdoor access and environmental education; explore outdoor careers.
i_can_statements: I can design and lead outdoor education programs that support learning outcomes — environmental knowledge, skills development, or personal growth.
I can understand and apply principles of transformative outdoor learning — using wilderness challenge to catalyze personal and social growth.
I can advocate for outdoor education access and equitable opportunities for diverse community members to engage with nature.
I can explore career pathways in outdoor education, environmental science, wilderness therapy, recreation management, and environmental conservation.
I can articulate how outdoor experiences have shaped my values, worldview, and sense of purpose, and how I might contribute to outdoor leadership.

competencies.raw: Develop, refine, and apply fundamental movement skills in various physical activities; develop and apply movement concepts and strategies in different activities; apply methods for monitoring and adjusting exertion levels; demonstrate safety, fair play, and leadership in physical activities; identify preferred types of physical activity.
i_can_statements: I can apply sophisticated movement skills and strategies across a diverse range of physical activities, including lifetime and recreational activities.
I can analyze and teach movement concepts and tactical strategies to others.
I can design and implement a personal training monitoring system that guides safe and effective fitness development.
I can lead and model excellence in safety, fair play, and sportsmanship as a senior student and community member.
I can articulate a detailed, realistic personal physical activity plan for post-graduation life.

competencies.raw: Participate daily in fitness-enhancing physical activity; describe how school, home, and community activities influence health; develop strategies for promoting healthy eating choices; assess factors influencing healthy choices; identify and analyze health message sources and their behavioural influence; identify and apply personal healthy-living goal strategies; reflect on outcomes and assess personal goal strategies.
i_can_statements: I can independently sustain a fitness program aligned with evidence-based guidelines for adult health.
I can synthesize knowledge of health determinants into a coherent understanding of how lifestyle, environment, and society shape wellness.
I can develop a comprehensive, realistic nutrition strategy for post-graduation life, accounting for budget, time, and social context.
I can critically deconstruct wellness culture, health trends, and commercial health messaging using research literacy.
I can create and present a comprehensive personal wellness plan for post-graduation life, integrating all dimensions of health.

competencies.raw: Propose strategies for avoiding and/or responding to unsafe or exploitative situations; propose strategies for responding to discrimination, stereotyping, and bullying; propose strategies for developing and maintaining healthy relationships; create strategies for promoting school and community well-being.
i_can_statements: I can lead others in recognizing and responding to unsafe, coercive, and exploitative situations with confidence and effectiveness.
I can take a leadership role in challenging discrimination, stereotyping, and oppression in my school and community.
I can mentor peers in developing healthy relationship skills and support those experiencing relationship difficulties.
I can design, lead, and evaluate meaningful health promotion initiatives within my school or community.

competencies.raw: Describe and assess strategies for promoting mental well-being; describe and assess strategies for managing mental health and substance use problems; create and assess strategies for managing physical, emotional, and social changes during puberty; explore and describe transition and change impacts on identities.
i_can_statements: I can evaluate and advocate for evidence-based mental well-being strategies at individual, school, and community levels.
I can demonstrate sophisticated mental health literacy — accurately describing disorders, treatments, and support systems — and apply it in peer advocacy.
I can design and evaluate a personal mental wellness plan that addresses the unique stressors of post-graduation transition.
I can articulate how my identity, experiences, and values will shape my well-being and relationships in adult life.

competencies.raw: Demonstrate sustained intellectual curiosity; formulate multiple hypotheses about scientific topics.
i_can_statements: I can formulate complex questions about human anatomy, physiology, and health.
I can generate multiple hypotheses about homeostatic mechanisms and organ system interactions.
I can predict the physiological consequences of disruptions to homeostasis.

competencies.raw: Collaboratively design investigations using appropriate methods and SI units while assessing risks.
i_can_statements: I can design investigations about physiological processes using appropriate ethical standards and methods.
I can conduct physiological measurements (blood pressure, heart rate, lung capacity) safely and accurately.
I can analyze anatomical specimens and models with appropriate methods and respect.

categories.name: Processing and Analyzing Data and Information

competencies.raw: Seek patterns in data; construct graphs; draw evidence-based conclusions.
i_can_statements: I can identify patterns in physiological data and connect them to underlying biological mechanisms.
I can construct and interpret diagrams of feedback loops, metabolic pathways, and organ system interactions.
I can draw evidence-based conclusions about health, disease, and physiological function from data.

competencies.raw: Assess methods for errors; evaluate model validity; critically analyze information sources.
i_can_statements: I can evaluate the validity of anatomical and physiological models and their limitations.
I can critically analyze health information sources including medical research and media claims.
I can consider the ethical implications of biotechnology, genomics, and medical research.

competencies.raw: Implement strategies to solve problems with local or global applications.
i_can_statements: I can apply anatomical and physiological knowledge to understand health conditions and treatments.
I can connect my learning to health science careers and real-world medical and public health challenges.
I can consider Indigenous healing practices alongside biomedical approaches to health and well-being.

competencies.raw: Present scientific ideas using evidence-based arguments and appropriate scientific language.
i_can_statements: I can communicate anatomical and physiological concepts using correct scientific terminology.
I can present evidence-based arguments about health, disease, and physiological mechanisms.
I can create diagrams, models, and reports that represent anatomical and physiological systems accurately.

competencies.raw: Demonstrate a sustained intellectual curiosity about a scientific topic or problem of personal interest; make observations aimed at identifying their own questions, including increasingly complex ones, about the natural world; formulate multiple hypotheses and predict multiple outcomes.
i_can_statements: I can identify and develop complex questions about biological systems and phenomena.
I can formulate testable hypotheses with multiple predicted outcomes based on biological knowledge.
I can connect my scientific curiosity to real-world biological problems in medicine, genetics, and ecology.

competencies.raw: Collaboratively and individually plan, select, and use appropriate investigation methods, including field work and lab experiments, to collect reliable data; assess risks and address ethical, cultural, and environmental issues associated with their proposed methods; use appropriate SI units and apply significant figures conventions; and record and organize data using a range of tools.
i_can_statements: I can independently design controlled experiments in biology, identifying variables and selecting appropriate methods.
I can apply ethical frameworks to biological research — particularly in genetics, human subjects, and environmental work.
I can collect and record precise biological data using laboratory instruments and digital tools.
I can apply significant figures, SI units, and proper lab safety protocols consistently.

competencies.raw: Experience and interpret the local environment; apply First Peoples perspectives and knowledge, other ways of knowing, and local knowledge as sources of information; seek and analyze patterns, trends, and connections in data, including describing relationships between variables, and identifying inconsistencies; construct, analyze, and interpret graphs, models, and diagrams; use knowledge of scientific concepts to draw conclusions consistent with evidence.
i_can_statements: I can analyze biological data sets to identify patterns, trends, and anomalies.
I can construct and interpret graphs, molecular diagrams, and biological models accurately.
I can draw evidence-based conclusions from experimental results and connect them to biological theory.
I can incorporate First Peoples ecological knowledge as a valid lens for understanding living systems.

competencies.raw: Evaluate their methods and experimental conditions, including identifying sources of error or uncertainty, confounding variables, and limitations; describe specific ways to improve their investigation methods; evaluate the validity and limitations of a model; and demonstrate an understanding of the importance of peer review.
i_can_statements: I can critically evaluate my experimental methods, identify sources of error, and propose specific improvements.
I can assess the validity and limitations of biological models and simulations.
I can understand and apply the principles of peer review and scientific reproducibility.

competencies.raw: Contribute to care for self, others, and community through personal or collaborative approaches; transfer and apply learning to new situations; generate and introduce new or refined ideas when problem solving; and contribute to finding solutions to problems at a local and/or global level.
i_can_statements: I can apply biological knowledge to real-world problems in health, medicine, and environmental stewardship.
I can propose innovative, evidence-based solutions to biological and medical challenges.
I can transfer biological concepts to new contexts including biotechnology, public health, and conservation.

competencies.raw: Communicate ideas, findings, and solutions to problems using scientific language, representations, and digital technologies; express and reflect on a variety of experiences, perspectives, and worldviews through place.
i_can_statements: I can communicate biological ideas with precision using accurate scientific terminology and representations.
I can produce well-structured lab reports, research summaries, and scientific arguments.
I can use digital tools to model, visualize, and present biological data and concepts.

competencies.raw: Sustain intellectual curiosity; make observations to identify questions; formulate multiple hypotheses and predict outcomes.
i_can_statements: I can identify complex questions about chemical kinetics, equilibrium, acid-base chemistry, and electrochemistry.
I can formulate specific, testable hypotheses for advanced chemistry investigations.
I can predict quantitative outcomes — concentrations, rates, pH — for chemical systems at equilibrium.

competencies.raw: Collaboratively plan investigations; assess risks; use SI units and equipment; apply accuracy and precision concepts.
i_can_statements: I can design advanced chemistry investigations with appropriate controls and quantitative methods.
I can handle hazardous chemicals safely and identify risks in advanced laboratory procedures.
I can collect precise quantitative data using burettes, pH meters, spectrometers, and electrochemical equipment.
I can apply significant figures and error analysis to advanced chemistry calculations.

categories.name: Processing and Analyzing Data and Information

competencies.raw: Interpret environments; apply First Peoples perspectives; seek patterns in data; construct and analyze graphs and models; draw evidence-based conclusions.
i_can_statements: I can apply First Peoples perspectives on chemical processes in nature (e.g., fermentation, acid-base in ecosystems).
I can identify patterns in kinetics data and use them to determine reaction order and rate law.
I can construct and interpret equilibrium expression (K) and reaction quotient (Q) calculations.
I can draw conclusions about equilibrium positions, pH, and electrochemical potentials from quantitative data.

competencies.raw: Assess methods and identify errors; improve investigations; evaluate model validity; exercise informed skepticism; analyze ethical implications.
i_can_statements: I can identify sources of systematic and random error in advanced chemistry experiments.
I can evaluate the validity and limitations of chemical equilibrium and kinetics models.
I can exercise informed skepticism about chemical claims in industrial, medical, and environmental contexts.
I can analyze the ethical and environmental implications of industrial chemical processes.

competencies.raw: Design projects with real-world applications; solve problems collaboratively; implement multiple strategies.
i_can_statements: I can apply kinetics, equilibrium, and electrochemistry concepts to solve real-world engineering and industrial problems.
I can design a chemistry solution or innovation addressing a health, environmental, or industrial challenge.
I can implement multiple problem-solving strategies including ICE tables, Nernst equation, and Hess's Law.

competencies.raw: Create theoretical models; construct evidence-based arguments using scientific language and conventions.
i_can_statements: I can construct and explain theoretical models for reaction mechanisms, equilibrium systems, and electrochemical cells.
I can communicate advanced chemistry concepts using correct IUPAC notation and conventions.
I can write laboratory reports presenting complex quantitative results and evidence-based analysis.

competencies.raw: Demonstrate sustained intellectual curiosity about scientific topics of personal, local, or global interest; make observations to identify questions; formulate multiple hypotheses and predict outcomes.
i_can_statements: I can identify complex questions about water security, climate change, land use, and sustainability.
I can formulate multiple hypotheses about environmental processes and predict probable outcomes.
I can connect personal observations to local and global environmental questions.

competencies.raw: Collaboratively plan and select appropriate investigation methods; assess risks and ethical issues; use SI units and equipment systematically; apply accuracy and precision concepts.
i_can_statements: I can design environmental investigations — field-based and laboratory — using appropriate methods.
I can assess safety, ethical, and cultural considerations in environmental research.
I can collect accurate environmental data (water quality, soil, air) using appropriate instruments and SI units.

categories.name: Processing and Analyzing Data and Information

competencies.raw: Interpret local environments; apply First Peoples perspectives; analyze patterns and trends; construct and interpret graphs; draw evidence-based conclusions; analyze cause-and-effect relationships.
i_can_statements: I can apply First Peoples ecological knowledge and land stewardship frameworks to environmental analysis.
I can analyze patterns and trends in environmental data — climate records, water quality indices, land use maps.
I can construct and interpret graphs of long-term environmental change, including climate projections.
I can draw evidence-based conclusions about environmental cause-and-effect relationships.

competencies.raw: Assess methods and identify error sources; improve investigation quality; evaluate model validity; recognize bias; connect to science careers; critically analyze information sources.
i_can_statements: I can evaluate the methods and limitations of environmental studies including sampling bias.
I can assess the validity of climate models and environmental projections.
I can recognize political and economic bias in environmental claims and evaluate information sources critically.
I can consider the ethical, social, and economic dimensions of environmental policy decisions.

competencies.raw: Contribute to community care; design projects with local and global connections; solve real-life problems; consider scientists' roles in innovation.
i_can_statements: I can apply environmental science concepts to design a sustainability project with local impact.
I can connect environmental science learning to careers in environmental management, policy, and research.
I can consider Indigenous-led environmental governance as both legitimate and effective.

competencies.raw: Formulate models; communicate scientific ideas with evidence-based arguments; express varied perspectives through place-based learning.
i_can_statements: I can communicate complex environmental issues using appropriate scientific vocabulary and evidence.
I can present evidence-based arguments about environmental policy to a general audience.
I can express how First Peoples perspectives and local landscapes shape our understanding of environmental sustainability.

competencies.raw: Demonstrate sustained intellectual curiosity; formulate multiple hypotheses about natural phenomena; predict quantitative outcomes.
i_can_statements: I can formulate complex questions about motion, forces, fields, and momentum in advanced physical systems.
I can formulate multiple hypotheses with specific quantitative predictions for physics investigations.
I can apply principles of relativity, circular motion, and field theory to predict physical outcomes.

competencies.raw: Collaboratively plan investigations; assess risks; collect reliable quantitative data using appropriate SI units and equipment.
i_can_statements: I can design advanced physics investigations with careful attention to variable control and precision.
I can collect quantitative data on forces, fields, and motion using appropriate laboratory instruments.
I can apply vector analysis and significant figures to complex physics data sets.

categories.name: Processing and Analyzing Data and Information

competencies.raw: Seek patterns in data; construct and interpret graphs and models; draw evidence-based conclusions.
i_can_statements: I can identify patterns in physics data and use them to derive physical laws and constants.
I can construct and interpret position-time, velocity-time, and force diagrams for complex systems.
I can draw quantitative conclusions about fields, motion, and momentum from experimental data.

competencies.raw: Assess experimental methods; identify error sources; evaluate model validity; consider social and ethical implications.
i_can_statements: I can identify sources of systematic and random error in advanced physics experiments.
I can evaluate the validity and limitations of physical models including special relativity and field theory.
I can consider the social, ethical, and environmental implications of physics technologies.

competencies.raw: Design cooperative projects with real-world applications; implement multiple problem-solving strategies.
i_can_statements: I can apply physics principles to solve engineering design and real-world technology problems.
I can implement multiple problem-solving strategies including vector decomposition, field diagrams, and conservation laws.
I can connect physics innovation to technological development in areas such as medical imaging, GPS, and energy generation.

competencies.raw: Formulate theoretical models; present evidence-based arguments using scientific language and conventions.
i_can_statements: I can formulate and explain theoretical models for fields, relativistic effects, and momentum conservation.
I can communicate advanced physics concepts using mathematical notation, diagrams, and correct units.
I can write lab reports presenting sophisticated quantitative analysis and evidence-based conclusions.

categories.name: Use historical inquiry processes and skills

competencies.raw: Use historical inquiry to ask questions, gather, interpret, and analyze ideas, and communicate findings.
i_can_statements: I can formulate meaningful historical inquiry questions.
I can gather and evaluate historical sources to build a reasoned argument.
I can present my historical findings in a well-structured and evidence-based format.
I can revise my interpretations when confronted with new evidence.

competencies.raw: Assess the significance of people, locations, events, and developments in 20th century world history.
i_can_statements: I can apply criteria (scale, duration, depth of change) to evaluate historical significance.
I can compare how the same event is viewed as significant by different nations or groups.
I can argue for a ranking of the most significant events of the 20th century with evidence.

competencies.raw: Assess the justification for competing historical accounts by investigating source reliability and adequacy of evidence.
i_can_statements: I can compare two competing historical accounts and evaluate which is more credible.
I can identify bias, perspective, and motive in primary and secondary sources.
I can explain what evidence is sufficient to support a historical claim.
I can analyze propaganda as a historical source.

competencies.raw: Compare and contrast continuities and changes for different groups at particular times and places.
i_can_statements: I can trace how the role of women changed across the 20th century in different countries.
I can identify what stayed constant in international relations even as specific events changed.
I can compare how different nations experienced the same global event (e.g., WWII, the Cold War).

competencies.raw: Assess how underlying conditions and the actions of individuals or groups affect events, decisions, and developments.
i_can_statements: I can identify the MAIN (Militarism, Alliances, Imperialism, Nationalism) causes of WWI.
I can evaluate how one leader's decisions (e.g., Hitler, Stalin, Churchill) changed the course of history.
I can trace the long-term consequences of events like the Treaty of Versailles or the dropping of atomic bombs.

competencies.raw: Explain different perspectives on past or present people, places, issues, and events.
i_can_statements: I can explain how the same Cold War event looked different from American, Soviet, and non-aligned perspectives.
I can analyze how colonized peoples experienced decolonization differently from European powers.
I can avoid imposing present-day values anachronistically on historical figures.

competencies.raw: Make reasoned ethical judgments about controversial actions in the past or present.
i_can_statements: I can make an ethical judgment about the decision to use atomic weapons, considering context and consequences.
I can evaluate the responsibility of bystanders and non-combatants during genocide.
I can argue whether modern nations have a responsibility to respond to historical injustices committed by their predecessors.

categories.name: Use Social Studies inquiry processes and skills

competencies.raw: Use Social Studies inquiry processes to investigate Asian history, culture, and contemporary issues.
i_can_statements: I can develop a research question about an Asian nation, region, or issue.
I can gather information from diverse Asian and Western sources and compare their perspectives.
I can present my findings in a well-structured written, oral, or visual format.
I can interpret demographic data, economic statistics, and geographic maps about Asia.

competencies.raw: Assess the significance of people, locations, events, or developments in Asian history and contemporary affairs.
i_can_statements: I can evaluate the historical significance of an event like the Chinese Revolution or Indian Partition.
I can compare how the same event is seen as significant by different Asian nations.
I can argue for the global significance of Asia's economic rise.

competencies.raw: Assess competing accounts of Asian history and current events by evaluating source reliability.
i_can_statements: I can compare Western and Asian sources on the same historical event.
I can identify political bias in media coverage of Asian affairs.
I can evaluate the adequacy of evidence for competing claims about a contemporary Asian issue.

competencies.raw: Compare and contrast continuities and changes across Asian nations and peoples.
i_can_statements: I can trace how colonialism changed a specific Asian nation's political, economic, and cultural trajectory.
I can compare how different Asian nations approached industrialization and economic development.
I can evaluate what has changed and what has persisted in a specific Asian society over the past century.

competencies.raw: Assess how conditions and the actions of groups affect events in Asia.
i_can_statements: I can identify the causes of a major Asian conflict or revolution.
I can evaluate the role of individuals (Mao, Gandhi, Mandela) vs. structural forces in historical change.
I can trace the consequences of colonial rule for a specific Asian nation's development.

competencies.raw: Explain different perspectives on Asian history and contemporary issues.
i_can_statements: I can explain how the same colonial period looks different from a Western and an Asian perspective.
I can compare how different ethnic or religious groups within an Asian country experience national identity.
I can analyze how Western media stereotypes or misrepresents specific Asian cultures.

competencies.raw: Make reasoned ethical judgments about actions in Asian history and contemporary affairs.
i_can_statements: I can make an ethical judgment about a colonial atrocity in Asia.
I can evaluate the ethics of authoritarian development models (economic growth vs. political rights).
I can argue for or against international intervention in a contemporary Asian human rights situation.

categories.name: Use Social Studies inquiry processes and skills

competencies.raw: Draw conclusions, assess positions, demonstrate leadership, identify problems, evaluate data, interpret maps/graphs/tables, cite sources, construct visual representations.
